executive_summary: The global network is experiencing critical pressure due to an absolute collapse in logistics resilience, characterised by a maximum supply chain stress score. This systemic vulnerability is compounded by significant physical developments in Taiwan that are currently outpacing public perception. Immediate intervention is required to stabilise distribution channels before energy volatility triggers widespread industrial paralysis.
correlations: The correlation between maximum supply chain stress and volatile Middle East energy spikes indicates that any further fuel disruptions will immediately cripple already exhausted global distribution networks.
